pred_reason: SWC-101 applies due to integer overflow risks in arithmetic operations like rTotal calculation without SafeMath. SWC-107 fits because multiple findings show reentrancy risks in state-changing functions like _transfer where state updates precede external calls. Evidence: Unsafe multiplication in decreaseAllowance (line ~208) could overflow rTotal; _transfer function updates state before Transfer event (lines ~208-218); Reentrancy impact includes bypassing rTotal limits and balance manipulation

findings.Issue: Unsafe arithmetic operation in decreaseAllowance function
findings.Description: Multiplication operation without SafeMath protection in decreaseAllowance function, identified in both smart contract code and static analysis results This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential integer overflow allowing owner to set incorrect rTotal values, bypassing transfer restrictions and undermining deflationary mechanism
findings.Location: decreaseAllowance(uint256 amount) function, line ~208: rTotal = amount * 10**18

findings.Issue: Missing zero-address checks in critical address assignment functions
findings.Description: Approve and setrouteChain functions lack zero-address validation, identified in both smart contract code and static analysis results This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Owner could accidentally or maliciously set critical addresses to zero, breaking contract functionality and transfer restrictions
findings.Location: Approve(address trade) and setrouteChain(address Uniswaprouterv02) functions, lines ~191-199

findings.Issue: Potential reentrancy vulnerability in transfer function
findings.Description: _transfer function updates state before emitting Transfer event, potentially allowing reentrancy if recipient is malicious contract, identified in smart contract code analysis This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Malicious contracts could reenter during transfers, bypassing rTotal limits and potentially manipulating balances
findings.Location: _transfer(address sender, address recipient, uint256 amount) function, lines ~208-218

findings.Issue: Unlimited token minting capability in increaseAllowance function
findings.Description: Owner can arbitrarily increase total supply beyond advertised maximum, identified in smart contract code analysis This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Complete devaluation of token by bypassing deflationary mechanism and fixed supply promise

findings.Description: Standard approve function implementation susceptible to front-running attacks, identified in smart contract code analysis This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Malicious spenders can front-run approval reductions to obtain more tokens than intended
findings.Location: approve(address spender, uint256 amount) function, lines ~213-216

findings.Issue: Front-running vulnerability in transaction limit mechanism
findings.Description: Simple amount-based transaction limit can be bypassed through multiple smaller transactions, identified in smart contract code analysis This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Bypass of deflationary burn mechanism allowing large holders to dump tokens without restrictions

findings.Issue: Missing event emission for critical state changes
findings.Description: decreaseAllowance function modifies rTotal without emitting event, identified in static analysis results This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Reduced transparency and inability to track important parameter changes off-chain
findings.Location: decreaseAllowance(uint256 amount) function, static analysis finding: events-maths

findings.Description: _transfer function allows zero-value transfers, identified in smart contract code analysis This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Unnecessary gas consumption and event logging without meaningful state changes
